code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Strong dissatisfaction or immediate product failure
    - Safety hazards or defective items that require return
    - Time-sensitive failures: "Broke within X days/uses"
    - Explicit warnings: "Dangerous," "Don't waste your money"

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Functional flaws override any positives
    - Products failing core purpose despite some utility
    - Regret language: "Wish I hadn't bought," "False advertising"
    - Pattern: "Works BUT [critical flaw]..."

    3 Stars (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons without clear dominance
    - Conditional praise: "Good for..." or "Works IF..."
    - Temporary solution: "Better than nothing"
    - Non-essential flaws with basic functionality intact

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Explicit star deductions for specific usability issues
    - Minor drawbacks in non-core aspects
    - Maintained recommendation despite clear flaws
    - Example: "Deducting one star for X but still great"

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Explicit "5 stars overall" declarations override aspect scores
    - Multi-feature excellence outweighing single drawback
    - Transformative benefits: "Life-changing," "Exceeded expectations"
    - Emotional endorsement: "Worth every penny," "Can't live without"
    - Minor drawbacks mentioned but don't affect recommendation

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:
    - Final update status determines rating (e.g. "UPDATE: Broke after 1 week")
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" statements override any deductions
    - Multi-aspect praise with single drawback → 5
    - Safety issues → 1 regardless of other positives
    - Price praise + minor issues → 5 if "exceptional value" phrasing
    - Deducted stars + strong recommendation → 5 ("Deduct 1 star but still love")
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "Gets 4 stars for comfort but 5 overall - amazing features make this mattress exceptional!"
<Reasoning>Explicit overall rating override despite specific aspect score. Enthusiastic endorsement confirms 5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: UPDATE: Worked great initially but broke after 2 months. Not worth the price.
<Reasoning>Negative update overrides initial positive experience. Short lifespan indicates poor quality.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: These towels look luxurious but require 3 washes to become absorbent. Expected better at this price.
<Reasoning>Aesthetic appeal countered by functional deficiency. Mixed with value concerns.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: The blender makes perfect smoothies but overheats constantly. Not reliable for daily use.
<Reasoning>Core functionality compromised by reliability issues despite good results when working.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: Solves my jar-scraping problem perfectly! Deducting one star because handle isn't dishwasher safe.
<Reasoning>Functional excellence with specific convenience drawback. Usability impact warrants 4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "I'm taking off one star for assembly instructions, but this is still a 5-star product. Works perfectly once set up!"
<Reasoning>Explicit 5-star declaration despite criticism. Overall enthusiasm remains.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: Arrived with sharp edges and exposed wires. Dangerous design - immediate return!
<Reasoning>Safety hazard overrides any potential positives. Clear 1-star scenario.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: Exceeded all expectations! Transformed my kitchen experience without any complaints.
<Reasoning>Unambiguous enthusiasm with no negatives. Ideal 5-star example.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: Perfect for guest rooms! Sturdy construction and easy assembly, though slightly smaller than expected.
<Reasoning>Minor size issue doesn't diminish strong recommendation and functional praise.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (29 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 14
  gold 5 -> 4: 5
  gold 4 -> 3: 5
  gold 1 -> 2: 2
  gold 1 -> 4: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "I always hate not being able to get the last remains out of a jar or a can. This definitely takes care of that problem and with ease. Pour out what you can and simply use this to scrape the rest out. The reason I'm deducting a star is because it isn'..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I really like these potholders. They look great and do a good job of keeping hot pots and pans and other dishes out of the oven off the table and counters. They had a bit of a plastic smell when they arrived but after I washed them (they held up fine..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "I bought these mainly for sending leftovers. It is easier than sending my rubbermaid containers because I am not out much money if they don't come back."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "I like the Stasher Snack bag. It is the perfect size for snacks on the go. I also like that you can store, cook, put in the microwave or even in the dishwasher! This is great for small treats. Don't try to overstuff. You don't want the seal to get fo..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I like my Star Wars characters. I have a Darth Vader mug already and now I have the Chewbacca. The cup is nice and I like that it has the sash detail on the front and back so you know immediately it is Chewie. The mug can hold 20 ounces. As with most..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "This really is a beautiful mug. It's so beautiful I am thinking of just using it as decor rather than actually using it. It's not really big enough to use for my morning cup of coffee (I need a BIG mug for that), although it's the perfect size for my..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [3, 5, 5] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "We are both tough on dish towels and use them constantly. Not only do we cook 90% of our meals at home but we also use fresh fruits and veggies with pretty much every meal and that means a lot of washing, drying and carrying things around...why carry..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I would consider this 6 piece set to be hand towels. They are 16 inches by 30 inches. They are not the thinnest towels I have ever seen, and they are far from the plushest. I checked the description again and they have bath towels and wash cloths. Th..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [3, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 29 errors, 24 borderline out of 80 examples."}
